commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Settings color-scheme observation, pulses the native Settings window appearance during appearance changes, restores AppKit inheritance, and adds an unreleased changelog note.

Reproducibility: yes. open Settings on current main and toggle macOS appearance while the window remains open; source inspection shows no existing toolbar appearance refresh path, and the PR proof covers the visual before/after flow.
commands.review_followup: - No repair lane is needed because there is no discrete blocking finding; the remaining action is normal maintainer review and CI completion.

target.title: fix: keep Settings toolbar in sync with dark mode switch
target.body:
## Summary

Fix the SwiftUI Settings scene's native tab toolbar staying in the previous macOS appearance while the content has already switched.

## Fix

- Observe the Settings view's live SwiftUI color scheme.
- Pulse the matching AppKit appearance for one main-actor turn so the native toolbar redraws.
- Clear the explicit appearance immediately afterward so the window continues inheriting future system changes.
- Reuse the existing Settings-window lookup for appearance refresh and resizing.

The original implementation left `NSWindow.appearance` pinned, which made Dark → Light work but prevented the following Light → Dark transition. The revised implementation preserves normal AppKit inheritance.

## Proof

- `./Scripts/compile_and_run.sh`
- `make check`
- Autoreview: no actionable findings, patch correct (0.82)
- Peekaboo against the freshly packaged app on macOS 26.5:
  - opened the native Settings window in Dark appearance
  - switched Dark → Light with the window open; toolbar and content both updated
  - switched Light → Dark; toolbar and content both updated
  - restored the original Dark system appearance
target.author: hhh2210
